query_basis: Reviewed live official KanCare Home, Eligibility, and Appeals/Fair Hearings leaves now preserve Kansas Medicaid coverage, eligibility rules, and appeal routing on the current first-party stack.

samples.evidence_snippet: Welcome to KanCare. KanCare delivers health care to eligible Kansans. The live official page links to Eligibility, Appeals & Fair Hearings, Medicaid State Plan, CHIP State Plan, and 1115 Waiver Reports.

The Kansas Department of Health and Environment (KDHE) Division of Health Care Finance offers medical assistance. KanCare under the Medicaid plan is the largest program and the page preserves covered groups, general rules, and income/resource guidance.
The live official KanCare Appeals & Fair Hearings lane preserves grievance, appeal, and state fair-hearing routing on the same first-party Medicaid stack.

query_basis: The exact Kansas HCBS waiver eligibility leaf now returns only a 403 challenge and no reviewed waiver content is preserved on disk.
blocker_evidence: Reviewed 2026-06-21 live probe to the exact KDADS waiver eligibility leaf returned an Akamai-style "Access Denied" 403 challenge page.

query_basis: The old dhhs.kansas.gov DD root is dead and the reviewed KDADS DD replacement remains challenge-blocked.
blocker_evidence: Reviewed 2026-06-21 live probe failed DNS resolution for dhhs.kansas.gov/dd, so the old packet DD root is dead. Reviewed 2026-06-21 live probe to the exact KDADS root returned an Akamai-style "Access Denied" 403 challenge page instead of DD, waiver, or early-intervention content.

query_basis: Reviewed live KSDE Early Childhood Special Education leaf now again provides Kansas birth-to-three, Part C, KDHE administration, and local ITS referral context.

samples.evidence_snippet: All Kansas infants and toddlers (birth to age 3) are entitled to a free developmental evaluation to determine if they are eligible for early intervention services. Kansas Early Childhood Developmental Services (KECDS) within the Kansas Department of Health and Environment (KDHE) administers early intervention services under Part C of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA). Visit www.itsofks.org to find your local Infant Toddler Services of Kansas program.

query_basis: Reviewed live KSDE Special Education leaf now again preserves a role-pure IDEA Part B root with direct dispute-resolution and parent-rights leaves on the same official path.

samples.evidence_snippet: The Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) ensures a free appropriate public education (FAPE) to children with disabilities and the official KSDE Special Education page links to Dispute Resolution, Procedural Safeguards (Parent Rights), Special Education Due Process Hearing Decisions, and Special Education Formal Complaint Decisions.

query_basis: Kansas still lacks reviewed district-owned or county-grade education leaves, but bounded review now confirms the official School District Maps page exposes a live USD county map PDF even though the extracted text still does not preserve a usable county-to-district contract.
blocker_evidence: The live KSDE Special Education, Dispute Resolution, Parent Rights, Data Central Special Education Reports, and School District Maps leaves are again reachable. The official School District Maps page also exposes a live USD county map PDF at https://www.ksde.gov/docs/default-source/sf/2025-usd-county-map.pdf?sfvrsn=8ceea3ce_5, and bounded extraction confirms that it is not dead or image-only. But the extracted text still collapses into district numbers plus county names without a reviewable county-to-district join, and the packet still preserves no district-owned local special-education contact source.

samples.evidence_snippet: Bounded extraction from the live official USD county map PDF recovers Kansas county names such as Allen, Anderson, Bourbon, Cherokee, and Shawnee plus many district numbers, but not a reviewable county-to-district join or local special-education routing contract.

query_basis: Reviewed live Kansas DCF Rehabilitation Services Program Overview page now provides a role-pure statewide VR root instead of the old KDADS misclassification.
Reviewed first-party DRC Kansas evidence on disk plus live About/Get Help pages prove the statewide protection-and-advocacy role and help path.
Reviewed first-party Families Together evidence on disk explicitly states that it is Kansas’ federally designated Parent Training & Information Center.
Reviewed live Kansas Legal Services root explicitly identifies itself as a non-profit law firm serving Kansans.
Reviewed ABLE federal-crossover source remains intact in the program spine.
Reviewed SSA crossover source remains intact in the program spine.
Reviewed live official KanCare Ombudsman Counties in Alphabetical Order directory now exposes county-specific Community Resources guide links across all 105 Kansas counties on the first-party stack.

samples.evidence_snippet: The official KanCare Ombudsman Counties in Alphabetical Order page lists county-specific Community Resources guides and shows 1 - 25 of 105 items, beginning with Allen County Community Resources through Edwards County Community Resources.
The same official directory continues county-specific Community Resources guides on page 2 and shows 26 - 50 of 105 items, including Ellis, Finney, Franklin, Geary, Harvey, and Johnson counties.
The official directory ends with county-specific Community Resources guides for Washington, Wichita, Wilson, Woodson, and Wyandotte counties and shows 101 - 105 of 105 items.
